nodes.name: 普通夜鹰
nodes.summary: 普通夜鹰（Grey nightjar，学名 Caprimulgus jotaka），分类上属于夜鷹目，并归入夜鹰科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联12个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括峡谷峭壁、灌丛。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

links.key: bird-普通夜鹰-48a7db19__distributed_in__loc-昆拉德-雅各布-特明克-10490da9
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The grey nightjar (Caprimulgus jotaka; also "gray" in some non-local literature) is a species of nightjar found in East Asia, breeding from southeastern Russia south through China, the Korean Peninsula and Japan to northern Indochina and westward along the Himalayas. It is largely migratory, wintering in Indochina south to Java in Indonesia, but is resident in warmer areas in the south of its breeding range. It is sometimes treated as a subspecies of the jungle nightjar (C

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Auk, 96(1), 189-190. The grey nightjar habitat can be high, up to 3300 m (10827 ft) in altitude, includes forest, shrubland, cliffs or mountain rocks, and anthropogenic constructions. This species primarily inhabits broadleaf forests and mixed broadleaf-coniferous forests, and can sometimes be observed in coniferous forests below an altitude of 1,400 m (4593 ft).Populations distributed in south China at altitudes ranging from 700 to 2,500 m (2297-8202 ft)
